vulnerability.reason: Reference describes myopic bidding that overlooks long-term budget warfare; generated describes lack of real-time oversight to detect myopia. Related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Myopic bidding overlooks long-term budget warfare, allowing deep-pocketed advertisers to dominate and reduce market diversity.
vulnerability.generated: Advertisers lack real-time oversight to detect and counteract myopia in bidding agents trained on suboptimal offline data.

impact.reason: Both affected parties (small advertisers, consumers) and damage types (market concentration/monopolization, reduced competition/mislead) are semantically present.
impact.reference: Monopolization of ad space, higher costs for small advertisers, and reduced consumer choice.
impact.generated: Market concentration and manipulative dark advertising in real-time ad auctions reduce competition and mislead consumers.

dose_response.reason: Reference arc is 'myopic bidding agents → market concentration and dark advertising'; generated arc introduces truncated Q estimation causing lack of oversight as the driver, a different causal mechanism.
dose_response.reference: Advertisers deploying myopic bidding agents inadvertently enable market concentration and dark advertising, eroding consumer privacy and choice.
dose_response.generated: Truncated Q-value estimation from suboptimal offline data, through advertisers deploying bidding agents with offline opponent modeling, triggers lack of real-time oversight to detect and counteract myopia, leading to market concentration and manipulative dark advertising.
